title: This must-have miniature Macintosh retro dock gives your M4 Mac Mini a 1980s makeover equipped with a 5-inch HD display and M.2 NVMe SSD slot
summary: The Wokyis M5 Retro Dock Station transforms your M4 Mac Mini into a retro Macintosh setup. It features a 5-inch HD display, M.2 NVMe SSD slot, and additional ports. This dock enhances your Mac experience with a nostalgic twist.
insight: Retro Mac makeover brings nostalgic charm to modern tech

title: Sony increases prices for refurbished PS5 slims by $100 PS5 Fortnite bundle is out of stock, marking the end of new $399 consoles
summary: Sony raises prices for refurbished PS5 Slim consoles by $100. The move follows an increase in brand-new models' prices last month. The PS5 Fortnite bundle is out of stock.
insight: Gaming console prices surge, limiting affordability for consumers

title: 45 years later, earliest DOS source code transcribed from a stack of old printouts found in a garage code was open-sourced to mark 86-DOS 1.00 s anniversary
summary: Microsoft has made 86-DOS 1.00 open-source to mark its 45th anniversary. This release provides access to early operating system history. The source code is now freely available for developers.
insight: Preserving legacy code for future innovation and historical context.

title: Retailer selling broken RTX 5090 GPUs for as low as $1,760 GPUs were damaged during transport, but include all components on the PCB
summary: A French retailer offers defective RTX 5090 GPUs for $1,760-$2,000, requiring repair or recycling. The units come with transport-related damage but include all components on the PCB. Customers can choose between a random variant or an MSI Ventus 3X OC model.
insight: Affordable, repairable GPUs could mitigate high-end graphics shortages.

title: FCC votes to ban all Chinese labs from certifying electronics sold in the US due to national security concerns ruling would affect 75 percent of US-bound devices
summary: The FCC bans Chinese labs from certifying US-bound electronics due to security concerns, affecting 75% of devices.
insight: Security of US electronics under scrutiny due to foreign lab testing.

title: Enthusiast creates Peltier thermoelectric cooler from scratch impressive rig uses two 360mm AIOs, homemade DC controllers, and a custom loop
summary: A YouTuber created a Peltier thermoelectric cooler from scratch, but its effectiveness was limited, consuming over 300 watts of power yet barely cooling an RTX 3070 below ambient temperatures. The homemade DC controllers and custom loop were impressive features of the setup. Unfortunately, the results showed that thermoelectric cooling may not be as effective as expected for GPUs.
insight: Thermoelectric cooling may not be worth the power consumption for GPUs.

title: Celebrate Star Wars Day 2026 with these upgrades to your gaming PC setup May the 4th bring you peace and prosperity with brand-new peripherals, games, collectibles, and more
summary: Upgrade your gaming PC setup with Star Wars-themed peripherals, games, and collectibles. May the Fourth marks the perfect opportunity to turn your gaming space into an immersive Star Wars experience. Get ready to join the Rebel Alliance with these epic upgrades.
insight: Enhance your gaming setup with Star Wars-themed tech and accessories.

title: Gigabyte Z890 Aorus Elite Duo X Motherboard Review: CQDIMM support with Arrow Lake Refresh
summary: The Gigabyte Z890 Aorus Elite Duo X motherboard offers a balanced mid-range package with Arrow Lake Refresh support, native CQDIMM compatibility, and ample connectivity options, all at a sub-$280 price.
insight: Native CQDIMM support expands storage options for users.

title: We tested supercharging the RTX 5090 in PhysX games using an RTX 5060 as a secondary GPU SLI may be dead, but how much can dual GPUs boost performance in classic PhysX titles?
summary: We tested supercharging the RTX 5090 with a secondary RTX 5060 GPU in classic PhysX titles like Batman Arkham games to see the performance boost.
insight: Dual GPUs offer significant performance gains in legacy 32-bit PhysX games.

title: Apple warns Mac mini and Mac Studio shortages could last for months local AI boom and memory crunch drive demand beyond Apple s manufacturing capacity
summary: Apple warns of ongoing shortages for Mac mini and Mac Studio due to high demand from developers seeking high-memory systems for running local AI models and agentic AI workloads. The shortages are expected to last for months. Local AI boom and memory crunch contribute to the issue.
insight: High-memory AI demand outstrips Apple's manufacturing capacity and supply

title: Tech teardown specialist delids a Xeon with a blowtorch and hunting knife wood chopping block makes a worthy stage for the sacrifice
summary: The Hackinator delids an Intel Xeon Silver 4110 processor using a blowtorch and hunting knife, demonstrating an unconventional approach to processor disassembly. This method may be useful for specific use cases or educational purposes. The process involves careful handling and safety precautions.
insight: Unconventional techniques can reveal processor design details for educational purposes.

title: Redditor gambles $20 on a 4TB Temu external HDD receives a microSD card reader hot-glued inside a plastic box
summary: A Reddit user purchased a $20 4TB external HDD that turned out to be a microSD card reader, hot-glued to a plastic case, with a card inserted. The device's true nature was a cost-cutting measure by the seller. This highlights the potential for scams in online marketplaces.
insight: Online marketplace scams often involve deceptive packaging and labeling.

title: Banana Pi BPI-OM7 AI 3D camera pairs BPI-M7 RK3588 SBC with ORBBEC Gemini 2 depth camera
summary: The Banana Pi BPI-OM7 is a 3D depth camera that combines a Rockchip RK3588 SBC with an ORBBEC Gemini 2 depth camera, suitable for applications in 3D vision, robotics, and edge AI.
insight: Enables high-precision 3D vision and edge AI capabilities.

title: Artemis II with four crew aboard lifts off for Moon
summary: The Artemis II mission successfully launched with four astronauts on board, marking a significant milestone in the United States' return to lunar exploration. The Space Flight System and rocket propelled the Orion space capsule into space on schedule. This historic mission paves the way for future human exploration of the Moon.
insight: First crewed mission to the Moon since the Apollo era

title: NASA contractors supply an array of sensing tech for Artemis II
summary: NASA contractors are providing advanced sensing technologies for the Artemis II mission, including sensor fusion and radiation detection systems to enhance the crew's safety and navigation. These technologies will help gather crucial data and provide real-time displays of critical information. The contractors' innovations will play a vital role in the mission's success.
insight: Accurate sensing tech ensures crew safety in deep space missions.

title: This Early Access pirate game was quietly killing your SSD without you knowing
summary: Windrose, a pirate game in Early Access, was quietly causing SSD damage due to a bug. The game's procedural generation and gameplay mechanics led to excessive write operations, resulting in wear and tear on the storage device. This issue was only discovered after players began reporting problems.
insight: Unnoticed game bugs can silently damage your SSD.

title: The Pentagon just signed AI deals with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, and Nvidia - and cut Anthropic out entirely
summary: The Pentagon signed AI deals with major tech companies, excluding Anthropic. The move aims to stay competitive in technological advancements. AI tools will give military an unfair advantage.
insight: Pentagon prioritizes AI partnerships to maintain military technological superiority.

title: A $7 Raspberry Pi can do something Sony refuses to: give the DualSense wireless haptics on PC
summary: A Raspberry Pi mod enables wireless DualSense haptics on PC, appealing to gamers.
insight: Affordable wireless haptics for PC gaming becomes a viable option.

title: GameStop wants to buy eBay in an aggressive pivot to e-commerce
summary: GameStop is considering a potential $12 billion bid for eBay, a significant e-commerce acquisition.
insight: GameStop's aggressive pivot to e-commerce could reshape retail landscape overnight.

title: This is Why You Won't Buy a Steam Machine in 2026
summary: Valve's Steam Machine failed to become an affordable gaming PC due to rising memory and storage costs. This has made the initial dream of a budget-friendly option unattainable. The project's viability has been severely impacted as a result.
insight: Rising memory and storage prices killed Steam Machine affordability.
